rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Costs And Sanctions After Lita Olquins Failure To Appear At Deposition Pursuant To A Valid Subpoena [Code Civ. Proc. 1992, 2023.010 And 2023.030]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 23, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for costs and sanctions after Lita Olquin¿s failure to appear at deposition pursuant to subpoena is denied. Although Ms. Olquin passed away after her scheduled deposition date, there is substantial justification why she did not appear for deposition. Further, imposing sanctions in these circumstances would be unjust. (C.C.P. 2023.030.) The opposition of John K. Bice Co. Inc. was timely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)
